pattern: Scan every index once, compute the circular distance min(|i - start|, n - |i - start|) for each index whose word matches the target, and keep the smallest such distance.
hints: The array wraps around, so the distance from startIndex to any index i is the smaller of the direct gap and going the other way around the circle.
You only need one linear pass: whenever the word at index i equals the target, compute its circular distance to startIndex and keep a running minimum.
If startIndex itself holds the target the answer is 0; if no index ever matches, the answer is -1.

edge_inputs.description: the target sits closer going the wrap-around direction than the direct direction
a single-element array where the only word is the target
a single-element array with no match at all
two occurrences of the target are equidistant in either direction
the target is reached fastest by going backward through the wrap
startIndex itself already holds the target
the target never appears anywhere in the array

stress.description: random word arrays over a tiny alphabet with the target sometimes present and sometimes absent
